fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England. The West Indies won the inaugural tournament, defeating Australia in the final at Lord's.
India won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2011 on home soil, defeating Sri Lanka in the final at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai. It was India's second World Cup title after 1983.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most runs in Test cricket history, with over 15,000 runs, and is the only player to score 100 international centuries.
The Indian Premier League (IPL), founded in 2008, is a Twenty20 cricket league and one of the most-attended cricket leagues in the world.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
Lionel Messi won his first FIFA World Cup title in 2022, when Argentina defeated France on penalties after a 3-3 draw in the final in Qatar.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier club football competition, with Real Madrid holding the record for most titles.
Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi are the two all-time top scorers in football history, both surpassing 850 career goals for club and country.
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, the French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in history, surpassing Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal.
Serena Williams won 23 Grand Slam singles titles, the most by any player in the Open Era.
Wimbledon, first played in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only Grand Slam still played on grass courts.
Rafael Nadal is nicknamed the 'King of Clay' for winning a record 14 French Open titles at Roland Garros.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
The Uber Cup is the women's team equivalent of the Thomas Cup, first held in 1956-57 and won by the United States.
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton, at the 2016 Rio Olympics, and later won bronze at Tokyo 2020.
Badminton was officially introduced as an Olympic sport at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics.
China has historically dominated Olympic badminton, winning the most gold medals of any nation since the sport's Olympic introduction.
The NBA (National Basketball Association) was founded in 1946 and is the premier professional basketball league in the world.
Kareem Abdul-Jabbar was the NBA's all-time leading scorer for nearly 39 years until LeBron James surpassed his record in 2023.
The Boston Celtics hold the record for the most NBA championships, with 18 titles.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and is widely regarded as one of the greatest players in basketball history.
Basketball was invented in 1891 by Dr. James Naismith in Springfield, Massachusetts, as an indoor game for winter physical education.
